div1.entryFree:
Boot, vb., 1) to avail: it --s not to complain, R2 III, 4, 18. it --s not to resist, H6C IV, 3, 59. Transitively: it --s thee not, Gentl. I, 1, 28. it shall scarce b. me to say Not guilty, Wint. III, 2, 26. R2 I, 3, 174. H6C I, 4, 125. Tit. V, 3, 18. Per. I, 2, 20.
2) to present into the bargain: I will b. thee with what gift beside thy modesty can beg, Ant. II, 5, 71.
